Name: Maintainability
Description: How easily the system can be changed or extended over time.

Name: Readability
Description: How easily developers can understand the code intent and structure.

Name: Testability
Description: How easily the system can be verified through automated and manual tests.

Name: Reliability
Description: How consistently the system performs its required functions without failure.

Name: Performance
Description: How efficiently the system uses resources such as CPU memory and network.

Name: Security
Description: How well the system protects data and behavior from unauthorized access or misuse.

Name: Scalability
Description: How well the system handles increasing load by adding resources.

Name: Usability
Description: How easy it is for users to learn and effectively use the system.

Name: Single Responsibility Principle
Description: Each module or class should have one reason to change.

Name: Meaningful Names
Description: Names of variables functions and classes clearly communicate their purpose.

Name: Small Functions
Description: Functions should be short and do one thing well.

Name: DRY
Description: Do not repeat logic; extract common behavior into shared abstractions.

Name: Low Coupling
Description: Modules should depend on as few other modules as possible.

Name: High Cohesion
Description: Related behavior and data should be kept together.

Name: Encapsulation
Description: Hiding internal details behind stable interfaces.

Name: Defensive Programming
Description: Code anticipates and safely handles invalid or unexpected inputs.

Name: Fail Fast
Description: Detect problems early and stop execution close to the source of the error.

Name: Immutability
Description: Prefer immutable data to avoid unintended side effects especially in concurrent code.

Name: Logging and Monitoring
Description: Record and observe system behavior to support diagnosis and operations.

Name: Input Validation
Description: Validate and sanitize all external inputs.

Name: Dependency Injection
Description: Depend on abstractions and inject collaborators to enable isolation in tests.

Name: Separation of Concerns
Description: Divide the system so that each part handles a distinct concern.

Name: Open/Closed Principle
Description: Software entities should be open for extension but closed for modification; add behavior by adding new code not by changing existing code.

Name: Interface Segregation Principle
Description: Clients should not be forced to depend on methods they do not use; prefer many small focused interfaces over one fat interface.

Name: Clear Error Handling
Description: Use consistent and explicit error handling strategies.

Name: Code Reviews
Description: Peers review changes to improve quality knowledge sharing and defect detection.

Name: Static Analysis
Description: Automated tools analyze code for defects style and smells.

Name: Continuous Integration
Description: Changes are integrated and tested frequently to detect issues early.

Name: Test Automation
Description: Automated tests run regularly to guard against regressions.

Name: Refactoring
Description: Behavior-preserving changes improve internal structure and clarity.

Name: Coding Standards
Description: Team-agreed conventions for style naming and structure.

Name: Pair Programming
Description: Two developers collaborate at one workstation to improve quality and knowledge sharing.

Name: Observability
Description: Metrics logs and traces give insight into runtime behavior.

Name: Continuous Delivery
Description: Automated deployments keep releases small and reversible.

Name: Long Method
Description: Functions that are too long and mix multiple responsibilities.

Name: Large Class
Description: Classes that handle many unrelated responsibilities.

Name: Duplicate Code
Description: Same or very similar logic appears in multiple places.

Name: Long Parameter List
Description: Methods or functions with many parameters.

Name: Feature Envy
Description: A method uses data from another object more than its own.

Name: God Object
Description: A single object knows too much or does too much.

Name: Magic Numbers
Description: Unexplained numeric or string literals in code.

Name: Comment Debt
Description: Outdated misleading or overly verbose comments.

Name: Tight Coupling
Description: Components are highly dependent on specific implementations.

Name: Global State
Description: Shared mutable state is accessible from many parts of the system.

Name: Primitive Obsession
Description: Using primitive types instead of meaningful domain abstractions.

Name: Lack of Tests
Description: Critical behavior is not covered by automated tests.

Name: Inconsistent Error Handling
Description: Errors are handled in ad-hoc or unclear ways.

Name: No Logging
Description: Important paths lack logging making diagnosis hard.

Name: Inefficient Algorithm
Description: Algorithms do significantly more work than necessary.

Name: Chatty Interface
Description: Many small remote calls instead of fewer coarse-grained operations.

Name: Insecure Defaults
Description: Default settings favor convenience over safety.

Name: Poor Validation
Description: Inputs are not thoroughly validated or sanitized.

Name: Complex Conditionals
Description: Nested or hard-to-follow conditional logic.

Name: Mixed Concerns
Description: UI business and infrastructure code intermixed in the same component.
